claims.evidence_text: Day and night lengths were 14/10 h, with 25°C and 20°C temperatures, respectively.
claims.claim_summary: Wheat seedlings were germinated and grown at 25°C day / 20°C night temperatures.

claims.evidence_text: No seedling survived at -1.0 MPa osmotic stresses.
claims.claim_summary: No wheat seedlings survived osmotic stress of -1.0 MPa (PEG 6000).

claims.evidence_text: In case of wheat, the main threatening issue is the deficit water at seedling stage, mid season stress and terminal stress.
claims.claim_summary: Wheat is sensitive to water deficit at the seedling stage, mid-season, and terminal (grain-fill) stages.

claims.evidence_text: All the under tested varieties fall in the category of drought sensitive genotypes as their % reduction is greater than 30%.
claims.claim_summary: Wheat genotypes with growth reduction greater than 30% under osmotic stress are classified as drought-sensitive.

claims.evidence_text: SD-621 had maximum shoot length (13.38cm) followed by SD-222 (13.33cm) with 21.62% and 20.80% reduction as compare to control at -0.5MPa PEG stress.
claims.claim_summary: Wheat genotype SD-621 reached a maximum seedling shoot length of 13.38 cm under -0.5 MPa osmotic stress.

claims.evidence_text: Highest root length was expressed by genotype SD-222, i.e. 19.84cm which gradually decreased to 12.12cm and 7.20cm in T2 and T3 ,respectively
claims.claim_summary: Wheat genotype SD-222 had a maximum seedling root length of 19.84 cm (control), decreasing under osmotic stress.
